rxn_class_summary: Photoinduced chiral N,N'-dioxide/metal Lewis acid-catalyzed asymmetric dearomative [2+2] photocycloaddition of quinolines and indoles with bicyclo[1.1.0]butanes (BCBs) to give chiral dihydroquinoline-fused and indoline-fused bicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes (BCHs).

key_intermediates.name: Triplet excited quinoline-LA complex (long-lived triplet diradical on quinoline C5-C8/C2-C3 of indole)
Carbon-centered radical adduct after first C-C bond formation between BCB terminal carbon and quinoline C5 (INT2-Si1, cyclobutyl/quinolinyl 1,4-biradical bound to Zn)
Closed-shell dihydroquinoline-fused BCH product coordinated to Zn (INT3-Si1)
Indole/Tb(III)/L4-TqCPh3 triplet diradical (τ = 0.04 s) for indole substrates

electron_flow: Chiral N,N'-dioxide/Zn(II) (or Tb(III)) Lewis acid binds the 2-acyl quinoline (or 2-acyl indole) substrate, lowering its triplet energy and inducing a bathochromic shift so that 400 nm light selectively excites the LA-substrate complex to a long-lived triplet diradical; this diradical adds to the strained central C-C bond of the BCB to form a chiral cyclobutyl/aryl 1,4-biradical (regio- and enantio-determining step), which then ring-closes to give the BCH product and regenerates the catalyst. SET is excluded by Rehm-Weller analysis.
rate_determining_step: First C-C bond formation between the photoexcited triplet substrate-LA complex and the BCB terminal carbon (TS1-Si1), which is also the regio- and enantio-determining step.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; ESI-HRMS detected [A1+B1+TEMPO+H]+ (m/z 468.2856) and [A1+TEMPO+H]+ (m/z 327.2068), supporting radical intermediates from both quinoline and BCB.

probe_reagents.name: Isoprene / 1,3-diene triplet quencher
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of C1 reduced to trace, consistent with triplet excited-state pathway.

probe_reagents.name: O2 (air balloon)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield dropped from 80% to 32% (ee retained 98%), consistent with triplet quenching.

probe_reagents.name: Naphthalene (3 equiv.)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield reduced to 57%, consistent with triplet energy transfer quenching.

notes: SET (photoredox) mechanism was explicitly excluded: excited-state reduction potential of A1/Zn/L3-RaPr2(CPh3) is +1.17 V vs SCE, incompatible with BCB oxidation (+1.83 V vs SCE). Fluorescence lifetime increased from 3.76 to 4.21 ns and bathochromic shift on LA addition support selective excitation of the LA-bound complex. Quantum yield Φ = 0.04 rules out radical chain, supporting a closed catalytic cycle. Linear ligand-ee vs product-ee shows one ligand per active Zn. Regioselectivity (C5 vs C8 on quinoline; C3 vs C2 on indole) is controlled by the steric profile of the chiral pocket (extended/open/partly open) tuned by ligand bulk; DFT shows TS1-Si2 (C8) is 1.7 kcal/mol higher than TS1-Si1 (C5) due to steric clash with the ramipril framework, and Re-face is blocked by aryl ligand groups (TS1-Re1 disfavored by 1.2 kcal/mol). For indole/Tb system, C3 attack favored over C2 by 2.5 kcal/mol (48.3 vs 50.8 kcal/mol).
